test_name: document title is AdminDashboard (login)
angular_behavior: Document title in `src/index.html` is "AdminDashboard".
react_behavior: Vite `web-react/index.html` uses "Admin Dashboard (React)".

test_name: login page: favicon link present in head
angular_behavior: `src/index.html` includes favicon to `favicon.ico` at repo root.
react_behavior: React `index.html` has no favicon `link` element.

test_name: login: submit button is never disabled while idle
angular_behavior: The login form submit has no `disabled` binding; it stays enabled during the credential request.
react_behavior: `LoginPage` uses `disabled={formState.isSubmitting}` on the submit `Button`.

test_name: login: failed login error is not a live region (no role=alert)
angular_behavior: The invalid-credentials message is a plain `.error-message` with no ARIA live region.
react_behavior: The same message is rendered with `role="alert"` for assistive technology.

test_name: unauthorized: outer wrapper is .container
angular_behavior: visual difference
react_behavior: `UnauthorizedComponent` is wrapped in an outer `div` with `class="container"` (full-height grid).
priority: The React page is wrapped in `.error-page-container` from `error.css`.

test_name: not-found: outer wrapper is .container
angular_behavior: visual difference
react_behavior: `NotFoundComponent` uses the same `container` shell as the other standalone error pages.

test_name: forbidden: outer error shell is .container
angular_behavior: visual difference
react_behavior: `ForbiddenComponent` uses the `container` + `forbidden-container` structure.

test_name: forbidden: Logout is a p-button with routerLink to /login (navigate on click)
angular_behavior: The 403 primary action is a `p-button` with `routerLink="/login"` (and logout on click).
react_behavior: The 403 action is a `Button` with `onClick` to `logout` + `navigate("/login")` (no `routerLink` on that control).

test_name: payment orders: row open-details control has no aria-label (icon only)
angular_behavior: The row action is a `p-button` with only the external-link icon (no `aria-label`).
react_behavior: PrimeReact `Button` for open adds `aria-label` including the payment reference (e.g. "View PAY123456").

test_name: users: edit/delete action buttons have no aria-labels
angular_behavior: Icon-only `p-button` row actions in the user table have no `aria-label` attributes.
react_behavior: `Button` for edit/delete uses `aria-label` values that include the user email address.

test_name: payment order details: back control is a link with an icon
angular_behavior: form UX difference
react_behavior: The back action is a `routerLink` with an icon; there is no `aria-label` on the link.

test_name: sidebar: Home is the only nav link with aria-current when on Home
angular_behavior: `routerLinkActive` + `ariaCurrentWhenActive="page"` so only the active route’s nav link is `aria-current`.
react_behavior: `NavLink` for Home is given a static `aria-current="page"` in JSX (always "page" on that link).

test_name: sidebar: on Orders route
category: Home link is not marked current
angular_behavior: navigation difference
react_behavior: On `/payment-orders`
priority: the Home link does not carry `aria-current` when it is not active.
column7: The Home `NavLink` still has `aria-current="page"` when viewing Orders (misleading for AT users).

test_name: sidebar: Dashboard brand area is not role=button
angular_behavior: The brand row is a `div` with `routerLink` to home (not exposed as a button).
react_behavior: The brand is an interactive `div` with `role="button"` and `tabIndex={0}` (keyboard-focusable "button" region).

test_name: layout: main content region uses .content (not .app-content)
angular_behavior: `app.component` uses a scrollable `div` with class `content` for the `router-outlet`.
react_behavior: `App` uses `div.app-content` for the same column.

test_name: document title is Admin Dashboard (React) on login
angular_behavior: Browser tab text remains "AdminDashboard" from Angular’s `index.html`.
react_behavior: Vite `index.html` title is "Admin Dashboard (React)".

test_name: login: submit button disabled while a submission is in flight
angular_behavior: The submit `Button` remains enabled for the full login request (per Angular template).
react_behavior: Submit becomes `disabled` while the login `Promise` is in flight (`isSubmitting`).

test_name: forbidden: page Logout is a real button
category: not a link (sidebar also has Logout)
angular_behavior: navigation difference
react_behavior: The 403 view uses a PrimeNG `p-button` with `routerLink` to `/login`.
priority: The 403 view uses a PrimeReact `Button` with click navigation (and the sidebar is a second Logout/Login).

test_name: login page: no favicon link in head (angular sets favicon)
angular_behavior: Angular `index.html` includes `<link rel="icon"… href="favicon.ico">`.
react_behavior: React `index.html` has no `link[rel=icon]`.

test_name: unauthorized: error shell uses .error-page-container
angular_behavior: 401 uses the Angular `container` outer wrapper (no `error-page-container` class on the outer node).
react_behavior: 401 uses the React shared `error-page-container` full-page centring.

test_name: not-found: error shell uses .error-page-container
angular_behavior: 404 uses the `container` outer shell.
react_behavior: 404 uses `error-page-container` for the same message block.

test_name: forbidden: error shell uses .error-page-container
angular_behavior: 403 uses the `container` + inner `forbidden-container` without `error-page-container` on the root.
react_behavior: 403 content is wrapped in `error-page-container`.

test_name: payment orders: row open-details control has View + reference aria-label
angular_behavior: Open-details is icon-only (no accessible name) in the Angular table.
react_behavior: Open-details is exposed with `aria-label` including the order reference (e.g. "View …").

test_name: users: edit and delete have explicit aria-labels with email
angular_behavior: User row actions are icon buttons without `aria-label` in Angular.
react_behavior: Edit/delete buttons include email in `aria-label` text in React.

test_name: payment order details: back control has Back aria-label
angular_behavior: Back control has no `aria-label` in Angular (icon-only).
react_behavior: Back control has `aria-label="Back"` in React.

test_name: sidebar: Dashboard brand is focusable (role=button) for keyboard users
angular_behavior: Brand is not `role=button` and is not a separate tab stop as a "button".
react_behavior: Brand is `role="button"` and `tabIndex={0}` for direct keyboard access.

test_name: layout: main content region uses .app-content (not .content under app-shell)
angular_behavior: Main column is `.content` in `app.component`.
react_behavior: Main column is `.app-content` in `App`.

test_name: login: failed login error is marked role=alert
angular_behavior: The Angular error `div` has no `role` (not a live region by default in the template).
react_behavior: The same copy is in a `div` with `role="alert"` in React.
